ideal_answer_shape: Directly state the related tests found, or state that no bounded related tests were found.
For each test, include concrete evidence such as matching symbols, assertions, imports, fixtures, or markers.
Provide exact narrow commands from the repo root when evidence-backed tests exist.
Expose honest gaps when bounded discovery finds no tests.
must_have_rules: Only claim related tests when repo-visible evidence exists.
Prefer direct symbols, assertions, and test definitions over filename or comment similarity.
Separate tests found from verification commands suggested.
Do not claim coverage unless assertions or direct behavior evidence exist.
If no bounded tests are found, say so plainly and avoid invented commands.
negative_cases: Ranks comment-only evidence above a direct test definition.
Provides a pytest command for a non-existent or unsupported test path.
Omits confidence or source refs for related tests.
Says no tests found when direct matching tests exist.
Recommends only broad full-suite commands when a narrow related test exists.
